title: 🔬 DORA × Copilot [Edu] [v2]
description: Cross-analysis of DORA engineering metrics segmented by Copilot adoption cohort, revealing the impact of AI assistance on software delivery performance.

list.name: environment
list.label: Environment
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green) or synthetic seed data (amber).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(
  (SELECT
    CASE mode
      WHEN 'seeded' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      ELSE '📡 ' || COALESCE(NULLIF(source_label, ''), 'Live Data')
    END
   FROM data_mode ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1),
  '⚠️ No data loaded — run sync or seed'
) AS "Data Source"

options.content:
# 🔬 DORA × Copilot Cohort

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot impact on DORA performance**

## What it Shows
This dashboard compares the four DORA metrics side-by-side for two cohorts: developers with an active Copilot seat versus those without. It surfaces whether Copilot usage correlates with faster lead times, more frequent deployments, or lower failure rates.

## Why it Matters
Cohort comparisons provide the strongest available signal (short of a controlled experiment) for Copilot's impact on engineering velocity and quality. If Copilot-active developers consistently show better DORA scores, it strengthens the case for broader adoption and investment.

> ⚠️ **Attribution caveat**: Cohort membership is determined by active seat status (last activity within 28 days) — not by confirmed Copilot use on specific PRs. Selection bias may exist if higher-performing developers are also more likely to adopt new tools.

## How to Interpret
- **Lead Time / Cycle Time**: Lower values for Copilot cohort suggest faster code delivery
- **PR throughput**: Higher weekly PR counts for Copilot cohort indicate productivity gains
- **Lines changed**: More lines per developer in Copilot cohort may reflect reduced friction writing code

panels.description: DORA × Copilot Cohort — compares DORA metrics between Copilot-active and non-active developers.

panels.title: Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + bridge resolver + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `deployment_pull_requests`, `deployments`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Bar chart comparing median lead time for 'Copilot Active' vs 'Non-Copilot' PR authors. Built with a WITH copilot_users CTE.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **copilot_users CTE** — defined as SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'. This is always a fixed 28-day window, not your Grafana time range.
- **Bridge resolver required** — only PRs linked to deployments are included; unlinked PRs don't contribute to either cohort's lead time.
- **Selection bias** — senior developers often get Copilot seats first; their faster lead times may reflect experience, not AI assistance.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Statistical significance of the lead time difference.
- Causality — this is observational; many confounders exist.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot.

panels.title: PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "PR Cycle Time (hours)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Bar chart comparing median PR cycle time (merged_at - created_at) for Copilot vs non-Copilot PR authors.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Calendar time** — wall-clock time including nights, weekends, and draft periods.
- **Cohort balance not shown** — the chart doesn't show N (number of PRs) per bar. If cohorts have very different PR counts, the comparison is unreliable.
- **Seat roster timing** — cohort uses current seat list. A developer who had a seat during the time range but lost it since then is now attributed to 'Non-Copilot'.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Statistical significance of the cycle time difference.
- Effect size — is a 2-hour difference meaningful?

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "PR Cycle Time (hours)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot.

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(*) AS "PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Merged PRs by Cohort (weekly) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=closed` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Weekly time series of merged PR counts split by Copilot/non-Copilot PR author cohort.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Roster timing mismatch** — today's seat list is used to attribute past PRs. A developer who got a Copilot seat last month is retroactively counted as 'Copilot Active' for all their historical PRs.
- **No per-developer normalization** — if the Copilot cohort has 10 people and non-Copilot has 50, raw PR counts are misleading. Consider normalizing by cohort size.
- **Mutually exclusive split** — the CTE creates a hard binary split; developers are never in both cohorts simultaneously.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Per-developer PR rate (normalize by cohort headcount for fair comparison).
- PR complexity or size differences between cohorts.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(*) AS "PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Merged PRs by Cohort (weekly).

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  SUM(COALESCE(pr.additions, 0) + COALESCE(pr.deletions, 0)) AS "Lines Changed"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Lines Changed by Cohort (weekly) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Weekly sum of (pr.additions + pr.deletions) for each cohort. A rename counts as additions + deletions (double-counting).
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **additions + deletions double-counts** — a 10-line rename (10 deletions + 10 additions) shows as 20 lines changed, inflating 'refactor-heavy' cohorts.
- **Auto-generated files** — PRs containing lockfile regenerations or generated clients have very high line counts with minimal developer effort.
- **Same roster timing caveat** — current seat list, not historical roster at merge time.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Code complexity or meaningful change volume.
- Whether large line counts represent valuable engineering work.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  SUM(COALESCE(pr.additions, 0) + COALESCE(pr.deletions, 0)) AS "Lines Changed"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Lines Changed by Cohort (weekly).

panels.title: Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
deploy_cohorts AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  dc.cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT f.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dc.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM deploy_cohorts dc
LEFT JOIN failures f ON f.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1

panels.description: Source: GitHub Issues (incident label) + GitHub Deployments API + Deployment-to-PR bridge + Pull Requests + Deployment statuses + Copilot seats | Tables: `deployments`, `deployment_pull_requests`, `pull_requests`, `deployment_statuses`, `issues`, `copilot_seats`

panels.title: Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

GitHub Issues (incident label) + GitHub Deployments API + Copilot seats

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `issues`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Compares change failure rate (incident co-occurrence / deployments) between deployers with and without active Copilot seats.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Deployer cohort, not author cohort** — cohort uses creator_login (who triggered the deployment API call), not the developer who wrote the code.
- **Incident sparsity** — with few incidents, 1 extra incident can swing a cohort's CFR by 10–20 percentage points.
- **No statistical testing** — raw percentages with no confidence intervals. A 5% difference may be entirely due to chance with small N.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Statistical significance of the difference.
- Causality — Copilot users and non-users may differ in many confounding ways.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
deploy_cohorts AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  dc.cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT f.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dc.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM deploy_cohorts dc
LEFT JOIN failures f ON f.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot.

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
failure_deploys AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.environment,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Median MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_deploys
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: MTTR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`, `deployment_pull_requests`, `pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Compares median deploy-gap MTTR (proxy) between deployments linked to PRs whose authors do and do not have active Copilot seats. Cohort assignment comes from the linked PR author (`pr.user_id`), not `d.creator_login`.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Same proxy limitation** — deploy-gap, not actual service restoration time.
- **Very sparse data per cohort** — splitting an already-sparse failure dataset by two cohorts means each bar may represent only 1–2 recovery events. Median of N=1 = that single value.
- **PR-link dependence** — cohort assignment only works for deployments that can be linked back to a PR; unlinked deployments cannot be attributed to an author cohort.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether Copilot helps teams recover faster or if recovery is driven by other factors.
- The number of failures in each cohort (denominator not shown).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
failure_deploys AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.environment,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Median MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_deploys
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for MTTR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Row count of copilot_seats table. Used as a data health indicator alongside 'Last Successful Sync'.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Silent 403 failure** — if the sync server receives a 403 on the seats endpoint, it logs a warning and continues. The table stays at 0 rows. A 'successful' sync job ≠ seats data was loaded.
- **TRUNCATE + INSERT every sync** — unlike PRs and deployments (incremental), copilot_seats is fully replaced on every sync. A 403 leaves it empty.
- **Proxy for sync health** — use this alongside 'Last Successful Sync' to confirm data freshness; check server logs if this shows 0.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether seat data is accurate or stale.
- Breakdown by team or repository.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Copilot Seats Loaded.

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API call) | Tables: `sync_jobs`

panels.title: Last Successful Sync - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API call)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

MAX(finished_at) from sync_jobs where status='success'. Shows when the last complete sync run finished.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **'Successful' sync ≠ all data loaded** — a sync job completes with status='success' while individual fetchers (e.g., copilot seats) fail silently. Always check records_synced per resource.
- **Timestamps in UTC** — finished_at is stored in UTC. Grafana displays in browser timezone; a sync at 00:00 UTC appears as yesterday in UTC-5 timezones.
- **sync_jobs is append-only** — each sync adds a row and the table grows indefinitely. No automatic pruning.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether all resources were successfully fetched (check records_synced column in the job record).
- Failure reason if the last job has status other than 'success'.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Last Successful Sync.
